message:
So last week our power went out for like six hours, and I watched my family group chat absolutely *lose it*. My mom asking if we had flashlights (we do, somewhere). My sister sending three different "is this safe?" links. My brother just posting the 🕯️ emoji over and over.

Turns out we had most of what we needed scattered around—but it took 47 messages to remember where. Which got me thinking: half the battle isn't *having* stuff ready. It's just... knowing what you have and where it is.

We spent an hour this weekend doing a real quick inventory with my kids—nothing fancy, just "okay, where are our flashlights, where's the water, what's our game plan if we actually need to leave?" Took maybe 30 minutes and somehow felt way less stressful than six hours of panic texting.

Has your family ever had that moment where you realize you're more prepared than you thought... you just didn't know it? 😄
